events.description:
Estimate first, then solve: 84÷4, 96÷6, 125÷5. For each, ask 'about how many?' before the exact answer. Write what the remainder would MEAN if 125 cookies are shared among 5 friends (none!) vs 6 friends. Ohio 5.NBT.

events.summary: [Reagan Homeschool] Long Division with Remainders — lesson + practice
events.description:
Use the steps: Divide, Multiply, Subtract, Bring down, Repeat. Solve 5 problems with 1- and 2-digit divisors, writing the remainder two ways (R and as a fraction). Then 2 word problems where you must decide what to do with the remainder (round up for buses, drop for full boxes). Answer space provided.

— dashboardBlockId=3000028 • Reagan Homeschool Dashboard (auto-sync)

Read chapters 4–6 of Tuck Everlasting (pg 19–36). Answer in full sentences: (1) How did the Tucks become unable to age? (2) Why do they take Winnie with them? (3) Predict: what do you think Winnie will decide about the spring? Book: Tuck Everlasting pg 19–36.

— dashboardBlockId=3000029 • Reagan Homeschool Dashboard (auto-sync)

Count out a handful of treats or beads. Share them equally among 3, then 4, then 5 'people' (cups). Write the division sentence and the remainder each time. Real-world remainders, no timer.
